steps.title: Use the highest ticket price
steps.content: The pass could cost less than individual tickets only as soon as the individual-ticket total can exceed $92$ dollars. To minimize the number of trips, use the highest ticket price, $3.25$ dollars per trip.

steps.title: Solve the inequality
steps.content:
Let $n$ be the number of trips. The pass is cheaper when:
$$3.25n>92$$
$$n>\frac{92}{3.25}$$
$$n>28.31$$

steps.title: Round to a whole trip
steps.content: The smallest whole number greater than $28.31$ is $29$, and that many maximum-price trips would cost more than the pass. The answer is 29.
